framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Build a public FP&A teaching platform
paths.what_it_is: Teach FP&A skills through a podcast, course, or community that reaches finance professionals well beyond your own company.
paths.why_it_signals: Paul Barnhurst, Carl Seidman, and Ben Murray each built national FP&A training brands — The FP&A Guy, Seidman Financial, and The SaaS CFO — out of practitioner FP&A careers.

paths.what_it_is: Build and share standardized metrics benchmarks — retention, CAC, Rule of 40 — that the industry uses to grade its own performance.
paths.why_it_signals: Ray Rike, Alex Clayton, and Dave Kellogg each built widely-cited public SaaS benchmarking practices that FP&A teams and boards now reference directly in planning and fundraising.

paths.path: Author or teach the field's formal curriculum
paths.what_it_is: Attach your expertise to a university-affiliated FP&A program or a reference book adopted as a desk standard, not just internal training decks.
paths.why_it_signals: Christian Wattig (director of Wharton's FP&A certificate program), Glenn Hopper (Duke Fuqua and Corporate Finance Institute faculty and author), and Jack Alexander (Babson lecturer and Wiley reference-book author) each turned personal expertise into a credentialed teaching institution.
